plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-256 RP07.P05.M06.S19-RP07.P06.M02.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-256 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP07.P05 fixture tail (M06 tail rows, M07-M10 fixture cycles) and opens the RP07.P06 permission/audit foundation (M00-M02) as eight descriptor sections over 150 planned rows, including permission matrix rows, decision bindings, audit hint fields, matched rule capture, deny-over-allow checks, legal hold/ethical wall/object ACL interactions, review/approval routes, security trimming proofs, audit event expectations, permission fixtures, and allowed/denied tests, with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-257 handoff, without opening runtime permission evaluation, audit event writes, AI runtime dispatch, search/OCR/index/embedding runtime, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-256 is a generated descriptor-only pack (RP07 search-core, 150 units, range RP07.P05.M06.S19-RP07.P06.M02.S20, risk C) closing the RP07.P05 fixture tail (M06-M10) and opening the RP07.P06 permission/audit foundation (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition). The pack-level production_ready claim is appropriately scoped and supported: command-evidence.json records production_ready=false with status "implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout", and every pre-review validation command (closeout-pack-plan:validate, rp05/rp06/rp07 validators, weighted/fullplan/spec/rp00-control-plane/goal:closeout validators, npm test, npm run validate, build, git diff --check, plus node --check on all changed JS) is recorded exit_code 0. The descriptor-verified production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && foundation.valid) is backed by the test suite and contract validator.

Verification performed via Read/Grep/Glob only: (1) Pack binding (registry.js:379), contract current_pack/cp256_requirements/cp256_no_write_attestation/p05_closeout_p06_permission_foundation_descriptor, README#cp00-256, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-256 at line 412) all agree on pack_id, risk C, unit_count 150, first/last unit, upstream CP00-255, next CP00-257, next_subphase RP07.P06.M02.S21, and identical deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions matching the prompt (implementation 58, fixture 28, claude_review 7, security_audit 23, test 18, hermes_evidence 4, ui 12 = 150). (2) No-write/no-real-data boundaries intact: freezeCp256Result (service.js:2817) sets all write/runtime/dispatch flags false, no_real_data true, deny_over_allow_enforced true, no cross-tenant/cross-wall access; the foundation validator (validators.js:4304) actively asserts descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no permission-decision/matched-rule/audit-body exposure, customer-safe errors, and no raw payloads across all 8 sections. (3) Claude remains read-only independent reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob; read_only true; claude_is_final_approval false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval false; claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false; human approval required for runtime opening at CP00-257). (4) Hermes patterns used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt identifiers (receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false) — no product-runtime execution. (5) CP178+ review hardening list (registry.js:562 forbidden_review_evidence) covers failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim. (6) Contract-to-code consistency is enforced by deep-equality assertions in validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:1064-1070, and the contract descriptor projection structurally matches the code output. All 7 required public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported via index.js. No premature production_ready=true and no weakening of production_ready gates. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-256 search core p05 closeout p06 permission foundation descriptor pack
